q: A friend asks a favor you don't have energy for:
options: I say yes and resent them later
I say yes but drop hints I'm tired
I say no honestly
I offer an alternative that works for me
Say nothing and stew internally
Laugh it off but feel hurt
Address it directly
Cut them off without explanation

q: How do you feel about saying no?
options: Physically painful
I can do it but I feel guilty for days
Uncomfortable but necessary
Easy — no is a complete sentence
Drained — I gave too much
Fine but I masked the whole time
Energized — I showed up as myself
Relieved it's over
I'll do anything to fix it
I obsess over what I did wrong
I hold space for their feelings and mine
Their problem, not mine

q: You overhear someone talking about you:
options: Devastated — I need everyone to like me
Pretend I didn't hear it, think about it for weeks
Curious but it doesn't define me
Don't care at all

q: How much do you share with new people?
options: Everything — I'm an open book from minute one
Too much, then I regret it
Gradually — trust is earned
Almost nothing — they have to prove themselves
Work bleeds into everything
I try but feel guilty logging off
I protect my off-hours
I keep work and life completely separate — no exceptions

q: When you need space in a relationship:
options: I don't ask — I just disappear for a bit
I hint at it and hope they notice
I communicate it clearly
I don't need space — I need walls
'I'm responsible for how everyone around me feels'
'If I set a boundary, they'll leave'
'I can love you and still say no'
'I don't owe anyone access to me'
